data.title: SAGT launches SAGE AI, secures $11M in enterprise contracts
data.description:
• Sagtec Global (SAGT) announced on Friday the launch of SAGE AI, its AI-powered restaurant operating system, alongside the award of initial enterprise contracts totaling approximately $11 million.
  • The firm said that SAGE AI integrates POS infrastructure, operational analytics, predictive AI models, and automated decision-support tools into a unified software platform.
  • "By applying machine learning and predictive analytics to operational data, SAGE AI enables operators to optimize pricing, forecast demand, manage inventory, and oversee multi-location operations," it added. 
  • SAGT +3.44% premarket to $2.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Insulet recalling certain defective Omnipod insulin delivery devices
data.description:
Insulet Corporation (PODD) has initiated a voluntary recall of certain lots of its Omnipod 5 insulin delivery Pods in the U.S. after detecting that some devices had a manufacturing defect that causes insulin leakage.

The recall came in response to reports indicating that the internal tubing of certain Omnipod 5 pods had a small tear, which could cause insulin to leak outside rather than being fully infused into the body.

Patients using the affected devices could risk experiencing high blood glucose levels due to insufficient insulin delivery, the Acton, Massachusetts-based MedTech disclosed in a statement late Thursday.

Insulet (PODD) added that it has received 18 reports of serious adverse events linked to the issue, and there were no deaths.

The company has already notified the FDA about the recall, which it said will affect nearly 1.5% of Omnipod 5 pod units it manufactures annually. The customers were advised to immediately seek a product replacement at no cost if a Pod from a defective lot is currently in use.

data.title: Core PCE inflation rises 3.1% Y/Y in January, a touch hotter than expected
data.description:
January core PCE (excludes food and energy): +0.4% M/M vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.4% in December, according to data released by the Bureau of Economic Analysis on Friday.

+3.1% Y/Y vs. +3.0% consensus and 3.0% in December.

The core PCE is important because it's the Federal Reserve's preferred measure to gauge underlying inflation trends.

Personal Consumption Expenditure Price Index: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.4% prior.

+2.8% Y/Y vs. +2.9% consensus and +2.9% prior.

Personal outlays: +0.4% vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.4% prior.

Personal income: +0.4% vs. +0.5% consensus and +0.3% prior.

data.title: Fuel shock creates buying opportunity for Knight-Swift Transport—Citi Research
data.description:
With increased fuel costs driving a risk-off trade in transportation stocks, the recent pullback in Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX) has created a buying opportunity, leading Citi Research to upgrade the stock to Buy from Neutral with a $64 price target that represents 23% upside from Thursday’s closing price.

While there are macro risks to the sector from higher oil prices, Citi Research analyst Ariel Rosa views Knight-Swift (KNX) as a Buy not only for the attractive entry point, but also for signs that truckload spot rates continue to come in above expectations, defying seasonal Q1 weakness. As the largest truckload carrier in the U.S., Knight-Swift (KNX) is uniquely positioned to benefit from “structural tightening in one-way truckload,” Rosa says in his note to clients.

Additionally, fuel surcharges and the record release of oil from strategic reserves should help insulate the trucking industry from the worst of the fuel shocks that have recently rocked energy-dependent sectors.    

“Knight-Swift, being the nation’s largest truckload carrier, arguably has the most cyclical torque to volume inflection as the end of the freight recession gives way to an upcycle. While we remain concerned about downside risk to near-term earnings given rate pressure, we think KNX and its management are in a good position to navigate the current downturn, with trough earnings limiting downside risks,” Rosa writes.

Shares of Knight-Swift Transportation Holdings (KNX) are fractionally higher into Friday’s open. The stock has gained 11% year-over-year versus -1% for Schneider National (SNDR) and -7% for Werner Enterprises (WERN).

data.title: SA analysts break down the market impact of the ongoing U.S.-Iran war
data.description:
As the conflict between the United States and Iran endures further, investors continue assessing the potential implications for global markets amid lingering uncertainty about how the situation may evolve.

With key questions around escalation, energy supply, and economic stability still unresolved, market participants are closely monitoring developments. Weighing in on the current conflict and its implications for the broader financial market are Seeking Alpha’s analysts. See below what they have to say: 

SA analyst James Foord indicated: “The war in Iran is disrupting markets, and indeed, based on recent developments, it seems like it could stretch out for longer. Equities may have a hard time in the next few months, and we could see some bottlenecks appear, but it's still early to call for an AI recession. Ultimately, I think this could be a good change to add exposure as the correction unfolds,” in Forget Oil: Iran War Could Eventually Trigger AI Recession. 

SA analyst Robert & Sam Kovacs said: “Assumptions of a quick resolution are misplaced; structural and leadership challenges in Iran make a near-term ceasefire highly unlikely… Market optimism underestimates the duration and complexity of the crisis; maintaining energy exposure remains prudent as second-order effects are not yet priced in,” on The Iran War Is Far From Over.

SA analyst Eugenio Catone stated: “Iran’s strategy centers on destabilizing global energy markets by blocking the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to induce inflation and economic strain on Western economies. I expect persistent upward pressure on oil prices and inflation, making long-term bonds, speculative stocks, and cryptocurrencies unattractive in this environment,” in Tehran's Economic Trojan Horse: Using High Inflation To Humble The U.S.

SA analyst Financial Serenity outlined: “Historical analysis shows energy inflation from geopolitical events is typically transitory and only marginally impacts overall inflation… At these levels, if the market were to force a mean reversion, it would have a downside buffer of about -7% to -10% from current values on the S&P 500,” in The Conflict In Iran Is Hiding The Truth About S&P 500. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: Oxford Lane Capital prices preferred stock offering for $60M
data.description:
• Oxford Lane Capital (OXLC) on Friday priced an underwritten public offering of 2.4M shares of 8.25% Series 2031 Term Preferred Stock at $25 per share, raising about $60M in gross proceeds.
  • The company granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 0.36M additional preferred shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on March 20, 2026.
  • The net proceeds will be used to repay outstanding debt, including existing preferred stock or unsecured notes, and for general working capital purposes.
  • Lucid Capital Markets and Piper Sandler are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering, while Clear Street, InspereX, and William Blair are lead managers, and Wedbush Securities is co-manager.
  • Shares -3.39%
  • Press release.

data.title: From pitch to portfolio: Goldman Sachs' top World Cup stock ideas
data.description:
Goldman Sachs thinks the FIFA World Cup in North America this summer could be a major catalyst for a wide variety of stocks. The first game is on June 11, and the championship game will be played on July 19. The U.S. will host 78 games, and Mexico and Canada will host 13 games each. 

Analysts think that consumer-facing names should see the biggest tailwind, led by travel, leisure, media, and beverages, as fans flock to host cities and tune in and gather in groups worldwide. Airlines, hotels, and booking platforms have already pointed to a benefit with more international travel booked this summer in comparison to last year. Meanwhile, advertising-exposed media groups and broadcasters could also profit from premium pricing on live sports inventory, while digital platforms capture brand spend tied to real-time engagement and betting. Payment networks and card issuers are also on the list of World Cup beneficiaries as elevated cross-border travel, ticketing, and entertainment spending flow through their systems. 

Among U.S. consumer and travel plays, Goldman Sachs pointed to names such as Booking Holdings (BKNG), Expedia (EXPE), and Airbnb (ABNB) due to strong exposure to accommodation and experiential travel in North American and European corridors. In the airline sector, carriers with robust transatlantic and intra-Americas networks, including Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL), and American Airlines (AAL), were highlighted as key beneficiaries of World Cup-driven passenger demand.

Hotel and casino operators such as Marriott International (MAR), Hilton Worldwide (HLT), and MGM Resorts (MGM) were singled out as well due to their historic trend of strong pricing power around major events and constrained room supply in certain host markets. In payments, Visa (V) and Mastercard (MA) are seen as clear winners. 

In beverages, Goldman Sachs singled out brewers and beverage giants with strong U.S. and European footprints, including companies such as Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD), Heineken (HEINY), Coca-Cola (KO), and Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP), noting that major football tournaments have historically coincided with higher on-premise volumes.

The Goldman analyst team also noted that Nike (NKE) is well positioned to leverage its scale, innovation pipeline, and wholesale relationships to capture disproportionate attention and market share within global football. Meanwhile, Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) and (ASO) were highlighted as having both started to incorporate World Cup assortment into their stores.

Other apparel companies leaning into sports that could see an incremental sales lift from World Cup tie-ins include Ralph Lauren (RL), PVH (PVH), and Lululemon (LULU).
